messages.content: hey, just wanted to check in. how's it going? haven't used one of these apps before lol
Hey! Good to meet you. I'm here whenever you need to talk—no pressure at all. What's on your mind today?
honestly just stressed about midterms coming up. got bio and calc in the same week which is brutal. my friends are stressed too so at least i'm not alone haha
That's a tough combo, but sounds like you've got good people around you. Have you started studying yet, or still in the planning phase?
started a bit but not as much as i should. been putting it off honestly. i'm usually pretty organized but this year feels different somehow? like i'm more scattered than normal
Being scattered around exam time is pretty normal, but it sounds like something's shifted for you. What do you think's different this year?

idk man. i think it's just everything combined. school stuff, trying to figure out what's next after this, my parents have been on my case more. and my best friend's been busy so i haven't really hung out with them in like 3 weeks
That's a lot happening at once. Missing your friend probably doesn't help when you're already feeling the pressure. Do you think reaching out to them might help, even just a quick hangout?
maybe yeah. i keep meaning to but then i get in my head about whether they even want to hang out or if they're just busy with other stuff. which is stupid i know
It's not stupid—that's pretty common when things feel off. But usually friends appreciate the invite. What's the worst that could happen if you just asked them?

honestly they'd probably say yes. i guess i'm just in a weird headspace where everything feels harder than it should be. even doing stuff i normally enjoy feels kinda meh lately. is that like... normal?

When stress piles up, things can feel flat and harder—that's pretty normal. But if it's been lasting a bit and nothing's feeling right, that might be worth talking to someone about. How long has it been feeling this way?
maybe like 2 or 3 weeks? i don't know. i'm not like, depressed or anything. just tired and kinda blah. my mom asked if i was okay yesterday and i didn't really know what to say to her

A few weeks of that blah feeling is worth paying attention to. Honestly, telling your mom what you just told me might help—that you're tired, stressed, and things feel off. She might have some good perspective.
yeah maybe. i don't usually talk to my parents about stuff like this but things have been weird between us anyway. i feel like they don't really get what it's like right now

Parents don't always get it, but sometimes being honest about how you're feeling can surprise them. Even just saying 'I'm struggling a bit' can open things up. How would you feel about trying that conversation?
Hey! So I've got this big calc exam coming up and I'm kinda stressed about it. Do you think it's normal to feel this way?
Totally normal! Most people feel exam stress—it actually means you care about doing well. How much time do you have to prep?
Like two weeks. My friends are all stressing too so at least I'm not alone lol. I've been studying like 1-2 hours a day which seems okay?
That's a solid routine! Consistent study beats cramming. You could bump it up slightly as exam day gets closer if you want. Are there specific topics giving you trouble?
Yeah derivatives are kicking my butt ngl. But my mom keeps telling me I'll be fine and honestly she's usually right about these things. Maybe I'm overthinking it?
Your mom sounds supportive! And yeah, overthinking can amp up the stress. Try focusing on one derivative concept at a time instead of the whole unit at once.

Good call. Tbh I've been sleeping okay and stuff so I guess things aren't that bad. Just one exam, right? I've got other stuff going on too—planning a road trip with friends for next month which is lowkey exciting me
Having something to look forward to is great for balance! Where are you guys thinking of going?
We're thinking BC—like maybe Whistler or somewhere on Vancouver Island. I'm pretty pumped but also kinda nervous about missing work shifts at my part-time job. Feel like I'm juggling a lot rn
Juggling is tough, but that's pretty normal at your age. Have you talked to your manager about the trip yet? They might be more flexible than you think.

Not yet. I keep putting it off honestly. Been feeling a bit tired lately and everything feels like it needs more energy than usual, even stuff I normally enjoy. The road trip still sounds fun but I dunno, just feels harder to get excited about it properly
That sounds like you might need more rest. Tiredness can make everything feel heavier. Are you getting enough sleep, and how's your overall mood been the past week or so?
hey, just checking in. how's it going? i've been meaning to talk to someone about school stuff
Hey! I'm glad you reached out. School can be a lot sometimes. What's on your mind?
honestly just the usual midterm stress. got three exams next week and i haven't started studying for one of them lol. my friends are all freaking out too so at least i'm not alone
That's pretty common this time of year! Breaking it into smaller study chunks can help. Have you thought about making a study schedule with your friends?
yeah that's a good idea actually. we usually do coffee study sessions and it works better than studying alone. my mom's been bugging me about my marks too which doesn't help lol
Parents can stress us out even when they're trying to help. Sounds like you've got a good system with your friends though. That's solid.
for sure. been trying to focus more this semester. i'm thinking about what i wanna do next year and it's kinda making me think about everything differently, you know?
That makes sense—thinking about the future can shift your perspective. What kind of stuff are you considering?

not totally sure yet tbh. been looking at programs but nothing's really clicking. like i know what i'm good at but i'm worried it won't lead anywhere. plus everyone has their stuff figured out already
It's actually pretty normal to not have it all figured out at your age. Most people don't, even if it looks that way. What are you good at?
writing and creative stuff mostly. but like, is that even practical? my parents want me to do something with better job prospects. sometimes i feel stuck between what i want and what i should do
That tension is real, and it's worth exploring. Creative skills are actually valuable in lots of fields. Maybe chat with someone about programs that blend both—that might help clarify things.
hey, just needed to chat with someone. been feeling kinda overwhelmed with school stuff lately. how's it going?
Hey! I'm here for you. School stress is real—sounds like you've got a lot on your plate. What's weighing on you the most right now?
honestly just midterms coming up. i've got like 3 exams next week and i haven't started studying yet lol. my friends are all freaking out too so it's kinda contagious
That's pretty normal—everyone's in panic mode before exams. Breaking it into smaller chunks usually helps. Have you thought about when you'll start studying?
yeah i know i should start soon. maybe this weekend? idk my brain feels like mush rn. do you think if i study like 3 hours a day i'd be ready?
Three hours a day is a solid goal, especially if you focus during those sessions. Quality over quantity matters. Taking breaks helps too. You've got this—you've handled exams before, right?

yeah true. last semester was hard but i did alright in the end. my parents keep asking how i'm doing in class and honestly it's kinda stressing me out more? like they mean well but the pressure is just a lot
Parents usually come from a good place, but that added pressure can def make things harder. Have you thought about talking to them about how you're feeling?

maybe... i haven't really talked to them about the stress part. they'd probably just tell me to work harder or something. my best friend gets it though, we've been venting about all of this together which helps a bit
Having a friend who gets it is huge. And hey, your parents might surprise you—they were young once too. Even just saying 'I'm stressed' can open things up differently.

maybe you're right. honestly i'm also just tired all the time? like even when i sleep it doesn't feel like enough. and i've been less interested in stuff i normally enjoy. is that like... normal or should i be worried

Some fatigue during stressful periods is normal, but losing interest in things you enjoy is worth paying attention to. It might help to talk with someone you trust—a parent, counselor, or doctor. How long has this been going on?
Hey! Just wanted to check in. How's it going? I've been pretty stressed about my midterms coming up next week.
Hey! That's tough timing with midterms. What subjects are you dealing with? Sometimes breaking study into smaller chunks helps with the stress.

Honestly just bio and calc are killing me rn. But my friends are all stressed too so at least I'm not alone lol. We're thinking of having a study session at my place this weekend. Do you think that's a good idea?
That sounds like a solid plan! Group study can help, plus you'll have each other for support. Just maybe set some ground rules so you actually focus and don't get too distracted.
True true. Yeah my friend Maya said the same thing. We'll probably do like 2 hour blocks then take breaks. My parents are being super supportive too which is nice.
That's really good to hear about your parents and Maya. The 2-hour block approach is smart. How are you feeling about the actual material? Do you have specific topics that are tricky?
Biology genetics stuff is confusing ngl. Like I get the basics but Punnett squares and whatever still mess with my head. Maybe I can ask my teacher for extra help before the exam?
Definitely ask your teacher—most of them appreciate when students reach out. Even just explaining it in a different way can make it click. You're already being pretty proactive about this.

Yeah I guess I am. Thanks for that. Honestly I've been feeling a bit tired lately too but I think it's just the stress and not sleeping great. Once exams are done I'm planning to actually relax for once lol
Sleep is huge when you're stressed—even trying to add 30 mins more could help your focus and mood. And having something to look forward to after exams is good. Any plans?
I'm thinking maybe just hanging with my friends, maybe a movie night or something low-key. I don't really have the energy to do much big stuff rn but yeah. Just want things to feel normal again soon.

That's totally fair. Low-key time with friends sounds perfect after everything. You're doing well managing all this—you've got a study plan, support around you, and realistic expectations. That's honestly a lot.
hey, just needed to chat with someone. been a weird week at school honestly
Hey there! I'm here to listen. What's been making it weird for you?
got my calc midterm back and did way worse than expected lol. like i studied and everything but just completely blanked during the test
That's frustrating, especially when you put in the effort. Test anxiety is really common though—have you thought about what might've thrown you off?
i think i just wasn't ready for how fast the questions went. my friend aced it which was cool for her but made me feel kinda dumb ngl
Comparing yourself to others is tough, but everyone's brain works differently under pressure. One test doesn't define how smart you are. What's your plan for next time?
idk honestly, been thinking about asking my teacher for help before the final. maybe some tutoring? im worried it'll be too late tho
That's actually a really proactive move. Tutoring before the final is definitely not too late—most teachers appreciate when students ask for help early. Worth reaching out to them?
yeah you're right. just feels like im behind everyone else on everything lately. school, college apps coming up, friends all seem to have their stuff figured out

I hear you—that pressure to have it all figured out is real at your age. But I promise most of your friends feel exactly the same way even if it doesn't look like it. What's one thing you *do* feel good about right now?
hmm, honestly my soccer team's been fun. we made playoffs and like, that's something i guess. havent felt like going as much this week but i probably should
That's actually huge! Playoffs are exciting. Sometimes when we're stressed we pull back from stuff we enjoy, but it usually helps to show up. How are you feeling energy-wise lately overall?
hey, just wanted to check in. been feeling a bit overwhelmed with school stuff lately
Hey! Thanks for reaching out. School can definitely pile up sometimes. What's been going on?
honestly just got midterms coming up and i haven't started studying yet lol. plus my friends keep asking me to hang out and i feel bad saying no
That's a pretty common spot to be in. Have you thought about which subjects you'll tackle first? Sometimes just starting helps with the guilt.
yeah good point. maybe i can study during the week and still see them on weekends? that could work i think
That sounds like a solid plan! Your friends will probably get it too. How're you feeling about the actual exams themselves?
i mean chemistry is gonna be rough but i usually do okay in english and history. just gotta buckle down for a couple weeks and i'll be fine
You've got a good handle on it then. Knowing your strengths and weaknesses is half the battle. Any specific topics in chem that're giving you trouble?
stoichiometry is kind of a mess ngl but my friend maya's pretty good at it so maybe i can ask her to explain it again. we haven't talked much lately anyway
That's a great idea! Teaching each other stuff is actually one of the best ways to learn. Sounds like you've got a good support system.
yeah maya's the best. honestly talking to you about this is helping too. i think i was just stressed and needed to get it out of my head
I'm really glad you reached out then. You've got this – you've already got a plan and people in your corner. Check in anytime you need to.
hey! so i've got this bio exam coming up next week and i'm kinda stressed about it ngl. any tips for studying?

Hey! It's totally normal to feel stressed about exams. Some things that help: break your study into smaller chunks, take breaks between sessions, and maybe study with a friend if that works for you. What topics are giving you the most trouble?
honestly the genetics unit is brutal lol. like punnett squares aren't making sense and i feel dumb for not getting it when everyone else seems fine

You're definitely not dumb—genetics is tricky for lots of people! Try looking up some YouTube videos that break down punnett squares step-by-step. Sometimes a different explanation just clicks, you know? And btw, I bet others are struggling too, they just don't show it.
yeah maybe. my friends are all saying they're ready but idk. anyway how's your day going lol weird question but i'm bored procrastinating rn

Haha I appreciate the question! Not bored here, but I'm glad you're taking a break. Procrastinating is rough though—maybe set a timer for 20 mins of study, then 5 mins break? Breaks are important but you gotta get back at it. You got this!
i know i know lol. okay imma actually study now. also btw do you think i should try the program at mac next year? im thinking business but not 100% sure

That's cool you're thinking ahead! Business at Mac is solid. Do you know anyone there you could talk to? And honestly, first year is about exploring too—you don't have to have it all figured out yet. What's drawing you to business?
idk my parents think it's good for job security or whatever. i like problem solving but sometimes i worry im not good enough for uni level work tbh

Those doubts are pretty common before uni, but getting into Mac means you *are* capable. And not liking what your parents want vs what you want is something to think about too. What kind of problems actually interest you most?
thats a good q. honestly i love helping ppl figure stuff out? like i'm usually the one my friends call when theyre upset. maybe something in like psychology or counselling could be cool

That actually sounds like a really natural fit for you! And it's awesome that people trust you like that. You could look into psych programs, or even volunteer somewhere to get a feel for it. What does your gut tell you when you imagine doing that work?
honestly it feels right? like more right than business does. but then i feel guilty for not following what my parents want and that stresses me out even more. it's all kinda messy rn

It's okay for things to feel messy—you're figuring out who *you* are, not just meeting expectations. That tension is real, but it's worth exploring. Maybe have a calm conversation with your parents about what you're actually interested in? They might surprise you. And remember, this is your path.
hey! so i've got this bio exam coming up next week and i'm kinda stressed about it. like i usually do okay but organic chem is destroying me lol
Hey, that's pretty common! Organic chem trips up a lot of people. What part's giving you the most trouble? Sometimes breaking it into smaller chunks helps.
mainly the reaction mechanisms honestly. like i get the concept but when i try to do practice problems i just blank. my friend maya says she uses flashcards but idk if that'll help
Flashcards could work! You might also try drawing out the mechanisms step-by-step without looking at notes first. That usually helps things stick better.
yeah that's smart. honestly i've been pulling kinda late nights trying to study which prob isn't helping my brain. been going to bed like 1am most nights
Sleep's actually super important for learning and memory! Even getting to midnight or 11pm would prob help you focus better during the day. Your brain needs that rest.
you're right, i know that logically. just feels like there's never enough time between school and work and studying. sometimes i feel like i'm just running on empty

That sounds really draining. Balancing work and school is tough. Have you thought about cutting back study time to get more sleep? Sometimes less focused studying is better than more exhausted studying.
i guess i could try that. it's just like... if i don't do well on this exam my parents are gonna be disappointed and i don't want that. they're counting on me for uni applications too
That's a lot of pressure to put on yourself. Have you talked to your parents about how you're feeling with all this? Sometimes they're more understanding than we think.
not really... we don't really talk about that stuff much. they just expect me to handle it. plus my older brother got into his first choice school so like... yeah. i feel like i need to do the same

Comparing yourself to your brother's path isn't really fair to you though. You're your own person with your own timeline. Would it help to focus on what *you* want for uni instead of what you think you should do?
hey! i'm just procrastinating on my bio assignment lol. how's it going?
Hey! I'm doing well, thanks for asking. Bio assignments can be a drag—what's the topic? Sometimes talking it through helps break through the procrastination wall.
it's on cellular respiration. honestly i don't even know where to start. my brain feels like mush rn
That's pretty normal—end of semester brain fog is real. Try breaking it into smaller chunks: maybe start with just the glycolysis part, then ATP. Small wins add up. You got this?
yeah okay that makes sense. my friends are all done their stuff already which is kinda making me feel behind but whatever lol
Everyone works at their own pace—you're doing fine. Plus, you're reaching out and making a plan now, which is honestly a solid move. How much time do you have?
it's due Friday so like 3 days. manageable i guess. been stressed about school lately though ngl. like everything feels a bit much
That's real—end of term hits different. Three days is definitely doable. Have you been taking breaks and getting enough sleep? Sometimes that stuff matters more than we think.
not really tbh. i've been staying up late and my sleep schedule is kinda shot. also my family's been on my case about grades and it's just... yeah

Family pressure plus sleep deprivation is a rough combo. Could you chat with them about what's realistic for you? And maybe try getting one good night's sleep before Friday? It'll help your brain actually process the bio stuff.
i'll try. i know i should talk to them more but i don't wanna disappoint anyone. feel like i'm supposed to have it all figured out already

Nobody has it figured out at your age—seriously. Talking to your family about real stuff, not perfect stuff, usually helps more than you'd think. And you're allowed to not have it all together. That's kinda the point of being your age, right?
hey! so i've got this bio exam coming up next week and i'm kinda stressed about it. how do ppl usually study for science stuff?
Hey! Bio exams can feel intense, but you've got this. A lot of people find it helps to break topics into chunks and use flashcards for vocab. Do you have a study group or prefer studying solo?
yeah my friend jordan and i usually study together, it's way less boring lol. we're thinking of doing practice questions this weekend. think that's a good idea?
That's a solid plan! Practice questions are super helpful—they show you what you actually know vs what you just think you know. Plus studying with Jordan makes it way more tolerable 😊
true true. honestly i've been feeling pretty tired lately tho, like even with decent sleep? is that normal during exam season or should i be worried
Pretty normal honestly—stress and mental load can def make you feel drained even when you're sleeping. But make sure you're eating okay and getting some breaks. How's your stress level overall?

it's manageable i guess? the exam thing is annoying but it's just one thing. mostly just tired. i've been thinking maybe i should go see my doctor to check if something's off, but idk if i'm overthinking it
If you're noticing persistent tiredness, it's worth getting checked out—no harm in that. Your doctor can rule things out or give you actual answers instead of wondering. Trust your gut on this.

yeah okay maybe i will. um also random question but like... do you think it's weird that i've been kind of avoiding hanging out with my friend group lately? usually i'm down for stuff but lately i'm just like nah
Not weird at all—sometimes we naturally withdraw when we're tired or stressed. That said, if it's a big shift for you, worth noticing. Have you talked to any of your friends about how you're feeling?
not really, i don't wanna be like 'hey i'm tired and weird' lol. plus they're all busy anyway with their own stuff. sometimes i wonder if they even notice or care

I get why you wouldn't wanna make it a big thing, but real friends usually do notice and care—even if everyone's busy. Maybe you could text one person, see what happens? And talking to your doctor might help you feel more like yourself soon.
yeah... maybe. thanks for listening. i'm gonna book that doctor appointment tomorrow and actually hit up jordan about studying. feeling a bit better about things now honestly
That's great to hear! Those are solid next steps. You're being proactive and that matters. Let me know how the appointment goes if you want to chat again 💙
hey! so i've got this bio exam coming up next week and i'm kinda stressed about it. do you think it's normal to feel like this?
Hey! Yeah, exam stress is totally normal – pretty much everyone feels that way before a big test. How much have you started studying so far?

not gonna lie, barely started lol. been procrastinating pretty hard. but i'm usually pretty good at bio so i'm hoping i can cram it all in. my friends are all freaking out too so at least i'm not alone
Haha, the procrastination struggle is real. Since you're good at bio, maybe try breaking it into smaller chunks this week instead of one big cram session? That usually works better anyway.
yeah that's smart actually. maybe i'll do like an hour a night starting tomorrow? also thinking of studying with my friend maya – she's really organized, so that might keep me on track
That's a solid plan! An hour a night is realistic, and studying with Maya sounds like a good move. You've got this – you've handled bio before.

thanks! honestly just talking about it makes me feel less panicked already lol. oh btw, i've been thinking about what i wanna do after graduation. my parents want me to apply to uni but i'm not 100% sure yet. kinda weighing my options
That's a big decision – it makes sense you're thinking about it. Have you had any conversations with your parents about what other options might look like?

not really yet, been avoiding it tbh lol. my mom's pretty set on the uni thing and i don't wanna disappoint her, but like... i dunno if i'm ready to commit to 4 years of school right after high school, you know? maybe a gap year or college could be better? idk
Those are all valid options worth exploring. A gap year, college, or uni – they're all legitimate paths. Might be worth having that convo with your parents soon, even if it feels a bit daunting?

yeah i know i should... it's just a lot of pressure sometimes. like everyone's got expectations and i feel like i'm supposed to have it all figured out already. sometimes i just wanna take a break from thinking about the future, you know?

That pressure's real, and it's okay to feel overwhelmed sometimes. You don't need to have everything figured out right now. Taking breaks from thinking about it is totally fine – you're only 18/19, you've got time.
Hey! Just wanted to check in. Been a pretty stressful week with midterms coming up. How's it going?
Hey! Thanks for reaching out. Midterm season can def be a lot. What subjects are you studying for?
Mostly bio and calc, which are my hardest ones. My friends are all stressed too lol. We're planning a study group this weekend which should help. Fingers crossed I don't bomb these.
That's great you've got your friends supporting you! Study groups usually help break things down better. You've got this. What's been the toughest part so far?

Honestly just the sheer amount of content, you know? Like I understand the concepts but there's just so much to remember. Also haven't been sleeping great which doesn't help. Been staying up late prepping.
Sleep's actually super important for studying—your brain needs it to consolidate what you learn. Maybe try wrapping up a bit earlier? Even 30 mins more sleep could make a difference.
Yeah I know you're right. It's just hard to stop when you feel like you should be doing more. My parents have been bugging me about it too which just adds pressure I guess.
That makes sense. Sometimes the pressure about studying becomes more stressful than the studying itself. What do you think would help you feel less pressured?

I mean... maybe if I just accepted that I can't know everything? Like I've been prepping for like 2 weeks already. I think I'm just in my own head about it. My friends seem way more chill and they're probably gonna do fine.
That's a really solid realization. You've put in the work already. Sometimes just accepting 'good enough' takes way more pressure off than chasing perfect.

Thanks, honestly. I think I just needed to vent a bit. Gonna try to actually get some sleep tonight instead of cramming more. Maybe go for a walk tomorrow too before the study group. Do you think that's a good idea?
Definitely. Fresh air and a walk will clear your head way better than more cramming. You're gonna do well. Let me know how the exams go if you feel like checking in after?
hey! so i've got this calc exam coming up next week and i'm kinda stressed about it. i don't usually worry this much but derivatives are just not clicking for me
That's pretty normal for calc! Derivatives can feel overwhelming at first. What part's giving you the most trouble—the concept itself or working through the problems?

honestly both lol. like i understand what a derivative is supposed to do but when i sit down to actually solve stuff my brain just goes blank. my friend said she finds khan academy helpful so maybe i'll try that
Khan Academy's a solid choice. Sometimes seeing it explained a different way makes it click. Have you thought about doing some practice problems before the exam, even like 10-15 min a day?
yeah i think i'll start doing that today. also planning to ask my teacher for extra help after class tomorrow. feeling a bit better about it already just from talking it through tbh
That's great! Asking your teacher is always a good move—they usually have tips that textbooks don't cover. You've got a solid plan.
thanks :) so apart from school stuff how's it going? do you find it weird talking to people all day? like doesn't it get exhausting
That's a thoughtful question! I don't experience tiredness the way you do, but I think what you're describing makes sense. Do you find yourself feeling drained after a lot of social interaction?

sometimes yeah. like after hanging with my group at lunch i feel pretty wiped even though i like them. my therapist says i might be introverted which honestly explains a lot. anyway i'm doing way better than i was like 6 months ago so that's good
It's cool you're aware of that about yourself. And it sounds like you've made real progress. That takes work. How are things going overall these days?

pretty good actually! i'm thinking about what i want to study next year and it's exciting but also like... what if i pick wrong y'know? my parents have opinions but i'm trying to figure out what i actually want
That's a big decision and it's normal to feel uncertain. What kind of stuff interests you when you're not stressing about school?
i really like helping people and i'm good with creative stuff so maybe something in counselling or design or social work? idk yet but i've got time to figure it out. feels good to have options
Those directions suit what you're describing. You've got self-awareness and you're asking good questions—that'll help you figure it out. Keep exploring and see what feels right.
Hey, how's it going? Just needed someone to chat with for a sec. Been a weird week honestly
Hey! I'm here to listen. What's made it weird for you?

So I've got this bio exam coming up next week and I've barely started studying. My friends are all freaking out too lol. Plus my parents keep asking if I need help which is kinda annoying but also sweet I guess
That's pretty normal pre-exam stress honestly. Breaking your studying into chunks usually helps. Have you thought about when you might start?
Yeah I was thinking tomorrow actually. Maybe I'll get my friend to quiz me or something. We could make it less boring that way. Do you think that'd work?
Definitely—studying with someone usually sticks better. Plus you'll keep each other accountable. Sounds like a solid plan.
Cool, thanks. Actually been meaning to ask—do you think it's normal to feel kinda tired all the time? Like I'm sleeping fine but just feel drained. Even coffee doesn't help much anymore
That can happen when you're stressed or not moving around enough. Have you noticed if it's worse on certain days?
Hmm, maybe? I dunno, it's just been this ongoing thing for like a couple weeks now. Makes it hard to get motivated for stuff even when I wanna do it, you know? My roommate's noticed too
A couple weeks of that's worth paying attention to. It might help to chat with someone—a doctor or counselor—just to rule things out. How's your mood been otherwise?
My mood's okay I think? I mean, I'm not sad or anything. Just tired and like... unmotivated? Still hanging with friends and stuff but I'm not enjoying it as much. That's weird right?
Not weird at all, but it's definitely something to mention to a doctor or school counselor. They can help figure out what's going on. Would you be open to talking to someone?
hey! so i've got this big bio exam coming up next week and i'm kinda stressed about it. do you think it's normal to feel like this?
Totally normal! Pre-exam stress is something most people experience. How much studying have you started so far?
not much tbh lol. i've been pretty busy with work and hanging out with friends. but i'm gonna buckle down this weekend, i think. my friend alex said they'd help me with the harder topics
That's a solid plan, and having study buddies definitely helps! Breaking it into smaller chunks this weekend should make it feel less overwhelming.
yeah for sure. honestly i've been managing pretty well with everything lately. school's been okay, work's been good too. my family's been cool about the exam stress which helps
That's great you've got good support around you. Sounds like you're handling the balance pretty well. How're you feeling about the actual material?
i understand most of it but like, some of the cellular respiration stuff is kinda confusing. plus i've been tired lately so it's hard to focus when i'm studying. do you think i should worry?
Cellular respiration can be tricky! And yeah, being tired makes it harder to learn. Are you getting enough sleep generally, or is this just exam week stress?

mostly exam week stress i think. but ngl i've been feeling kinda drained even outside of studying. like even hanging with friends doesn't feel as fun as it usually does? idk, maybe i'm just overthinking it
It's worth paying attention to that feeling. Sometimes a combo of stress and fatigue can dampen things. Are you taking breaks when you study? And getting outside at all?

not really taking breaks honestly. i just kind of push through. and yeah i haven't been outside much this past week. work and studying has been taking up everything. my motivation's been pretty low even for stuff i usually enjoy

Taking breaks is actually important for learning. Maybe try stepping outside for even 10 mins, or doing something you enjoy tomorrow. If this low feeling sticks around after the exam, it might be worth chatting with someone you trust about it.
Hey, just wanted to check in. Been a weird week honestly. How's it going?
Hey! I'm here for you. Weird how? What's been going on this week?
Mostly just school stuff. Got this huge assignment due next week and I haven't even started lol. Plus my friends are being kinda extra rn which is lowkey annoying
School stress + friend drama is a lot to juggle. What's making the assignment feel so overwhelming? Sometimes breaking it down helps.
It's a research paper and I literally have no idea where to start. I usually do okay with these but this one just feels different, harder maybe? Idk
That feeling of being stuck is real. Have you talked to your teacher about it? Sometimes just getting clarity on expectations makes it less scary.
Nah not yet. Honestly been avoiding it which I know is dumb lol. My mom keeps asking if I need help and I'm just like 'yeah I got it' but clearly I don't
